open_hooks_to_carry: The aftermath: Hogwarts in ruins, students mourning, the Great Hall as triage ward. The cost of the battle made specific through named dead and visible damage
Miren's visit: exhausted, scarred, not arguing. She tells him who died. He listens. The conversation is careful and halting — two people navigating the new shape of a changed relationship
prev_tail_excerpt:
...ople looking for other people in the rubble, its morning after a night that had changed everything for everyone except him. The world he was trying to keep.

He looked up from the manuscript. Through the window, the sky was pale blue at the horizon and darker above, the last stars of the night dissolving into morning. The grounds below were visible now — churned earth, scorch marks, a collapsed section of wall near the greenhouses. Smoke rose from somewhere to the north.

The wards were there, overhead, invisible to anyone else — a vast interlocking architecture that wrapped the planet, perceivable only through the substrate awareness the manuscript had built into his cognition. He could see them. They were thinner than they had been yesterday. They were holding.

Beyond them — past the wards, past the boundary of everything the wizarding world knew or could perceive — the thing that was not a thing occupied its space. Patient in the way erosion was patient. In the way a rising water table was patient. It would be there tomorrow, and the day after, and every day until the wards failed or held. It was not waiting for anything. It was simply there.

Gavin looked down at the manuscript. The notation on the page shifted in its slow representational cycle, the Builders' symbols showing him the coupling mechanism he'd just begun to understand, the key to the wards' self-repair, the first real foothold in a problem that might take years and might be impossible and that nobody else in the world was working on or would ever be able to work on.

He turned to the next page. He worked.
state_deltas_required: The quiet after the battle — grief, relief, rebuilding, the return of normal human concerns — pressing against Gavin's unchanged commitment to the cosmic problem that nobody else sees

Open ending. Miren visits, believes him for the first time, and leaves him to his work. Gavin is alone with the manuscript, the failing wards, and the beginning of the real work. The human story has ended; the cosmic story has barely begun
